title: SPIKE: does a PreToolUse payload distinguish orchestrator from subagent?
action: Answer ONE yes/no question with a real payload capture: can a PreToolUse hook tell an orchestrator's tool call apart from a dispatched subagent's? Write a throwaway probe hook under .bee/spikes/ that JSON.stringify's its ENTIRE stdin payload to a file (append, one line per invocation). Bind it on Read. Trigger one Read from the top-level session and one Read from inside a dispatched subagent. Diff the two captured payloads and report exactly which fields differ — session_id, transcript_path, cwd, or any sidechain/agent discriminator. A NO is a finding, not a failure: it settles permanently that no per-agent hook is buildable, and it narrows the tools-logger to a single undifferentiated stream. Record the verbatim field names present, redacting nothing except any absolute path containing a home directory.

truths: the spike file states a plain YES or NO
two real captured payloads are diffed, not reasoned about from documentation
the exact discriminating field is named, or the absence of one is stated plainly

artifacts.substantive: question, both captured payload shapes, the diff, and the verdict

key_links: Gates the tools-logger agent column (Slice 3, W4): docs/history/advisor-and-orchestration/plan.md
prohibitions: The probe hook lives under .bee/spikes/ and is never promoted to hooks/ — spike code is disposable by rule
Do not register the probe in hooks/catalog.mjs or any projection
Do not build the tools logger in this cell

trace.dropped_reason: Rebuilt after the high-risk validating panel: ao-0's verify could never pass (test_hook_contracts is also red), its falsifiability step contradicted its own prohibitions, and its verify-chain edit would have bricked the baseline; ao-s1's question was answerable yes-by-redefinition; ao-s2's binding step and capture shape were unsafe and its acceptance criterion was unsatisfiable on the most likely answer.
